query_normalized: predicting molecular polarity from smiles strings with machine learning chemistry
rationale: This paper satisfies criterion (a) as it measures the same physical observable (molecular dipole moment, the quantitative measure of polarity) using machine learning, and criterion (b) as it establishes an empirical baseline for predicting this specific molecular property, even though it focuses on dipole magnitudes rather than the specific input format (SMILES) mentioned in the user's query.
